title: Tagging image with multiple tags is broken when all layers cached
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Take any `Dockerfile`
2. Build it with multiple tags: `buildah bud -t tag1 -t tag2 .`

**Describe the results you received:**
Only the first tag gets applied:
```
$ buildah images | grep tag
localhost/tag1             latest   94e814e2efa8   2 months ago   91.4 MB
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Both tags get applied. See the output for older `buildah` (Apr 13) for comparison:
```
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
localhost/tag2             latest   fd28997279dc   5 seconds ago   72.3 MB
localhost/tag1             latest   fd28997279dc   5 seconds ago   72.3 MB
...
```
And Docker:
```
tag1    latest              a0270224c1b1        About an hour ago   88.9MB
tag2    latest              a0270224c1b1        About an hour ago   88.9MB
```
**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
Built from master branch
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.9.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.12.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
N/A
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=19.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=disco
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 19.04"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="19.04 (Disco Dingo)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 19.04"
VERSION_ID="19.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=disco
UBUNTU_CODENAME=disco
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux <redacted> 5.0.0-15-generic #16-Ubuntu SMP Mon May 6 17:41:33 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body: @EricHripko thanks for the problem report, we'll take a look at it.

Had another quick look - it seems that the issue occurs when cache is involved. I can easily reproduce by rebuilding the `Dockerfile` that's already fully cached:
```
$ podman build -f hello.Dockerfile -t tag1 -t tag2
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
STEP 2: COPY file1 file2 /bin
--> Using cache bd6f9d58aa2a2434fcc9fbafc97b046e80bb5d209acc65910e742781edf891c6
STEP 3: COMMIT tag1
--> bd6f9d58aa2a2434fcc9fbafc97b046e80bb5d209acc65910e742781edf891c6
$ podman images | grep tag
localhost/tag1             latest   bd6f9d58aa2a   4 days ago       91.4 MB
```
Oh, that makes a lot of sense.  When there's more than one name to apply, we currently do it in `Commit()`, which we don't call when we end up just tagging an image that's already there.
Looks like a fix for this was committed in master, Please test it out.
I am not sure how to make `buildah bud` utilise cache - will wait till `podman` picks up this change to test.

```
man buildah bud
...
       --layers bool-value

       Cache intermediate images during the build process (Default is false).

       Note:  You can also override the default value of layers by setting the
       BUILDAH_LAYERS environment variable. export BUILDAH_LAYERS=true
```

Oops, sorry about that! Should've read the manual more thoroughly :see_no_evil: 
```
$ buildah bud -t tag1 -t tag2 --layers=true .
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/ubuntu
STEP 2: COPY file1 file2 /bin
--> Using cache 49d1198c70dac2db9f8d2217a3cb6d337097fd8b4b6bfe2aca630711b1ec1f47
STEP 3: COMMIT tag1
--> 49d1198c70dac2db9f8d2217a3cb6d337097fd8b4b6bfe2aca630711b1ec1f47
$ buildah images | grep tag
localhost/tag2             latest   49d1198c70da   7 days ago     91.4 MB
localhost/tag1             latest   49d1198c70da   7 days ago     91.4 MB
```
It's indeed all fine now :+1:
